title: Worktree Diff Review with Sidebar and Annotations
context: Build a Neovim plugin for reviewing worktree diffs with a file sidebar, side-by-side diff mode, and integration with the existing review/init.lua comment system.
architecture: New wt_review.lua module that integrates with git_diff_review, review/init.lua, and wt CLI

red.description: Create tests/wt_review_spec.lua that fails because require('wt_review') is missing and resolve_base_ref returns wrong value

green.description: Create lua/wt_review/init.lua with module skeleton, resolve_base_ref using wt CLI + merge-base

red.description: Test that get_changed_files returns sorted list with +N/-M stats

green.description: Implement get_changed_files using git diff --numstat, merge untracked files

red.description: Test that open_sidebar creates a buffer with correct file list and highlights

green.description: Implement sidebar buffer with 'wtreview' filetype, render files with +N/-M stats

steps.name: Diff buffer setup for a selected file

red.description: Verify diff mode opens two buffers (working tree vs base version) with correct content

green.description: Implement open_file_diff that loads base version into temp buffer, sets diffthis on both

steps.name: Navigation keymaps for files and hunks

red.description: Test ]f/[f cycle through sidebar entries, ]h/[h use gitsigns nav

green.description: Implement file cycling with autocmds, hunk nav delegates to gitsigns

red.description: Verify review.add() receives correct original source line numbers in diff mode

green.description: Implement diff buffer line → source line mapping, wrap review.add()

red.description: Update wpi TUI review stage to call wt_review instead of git_diff_review

green.description: Replace git_diff_review.open() with wt_review.open(), set WPI_BASE_REF

red.description: Run :WtReview in a worktree with actual changes, verify sidebar + diff + comments

green.description: Fix any remaining bugs from manual test

design.currentState: The wpi TUI has a review step that opens nvim with git_diff_review.open(mergeBase). git_diff_review uses quickfix + gitsigns hunks for navigation across files. review/init.lua provides extmark-based comment annotations that save to .code-review.md. There's no file sidebar, no proper side-by-side diff mode for review, and review comments don't work in diff buffers because line numbers don't map. No Lua test directory exists.
design.desiredState: A :WtReview [branch?] command that opens a file sidebar showing changed files with diff stats (+N/-M). Selecting a file opens it in side-by-side diff mode (working tree vs base). Navigating with ]f/[f for files and ]h/[h for hunks. Comments via the review system map to actual source line numbers. wt CLI used for branch detection.
design.patterns: git_diff_review exists with quickfix-based review. review/init.lua uses extmarks and .code-review.md. wpi TUI sets WPI_BASE_REF and launches nvim with git_diff_review. wt config state default-branch gives the base branch.
design.decisions: Use side-by-side diff of actual source files (not unified diff). Reuse review/init.lua comment system. Use pure Neovim diff rendering. Hybrid navigation: sidebar for files + ]h/[h for hunks. Auto-detect branch via wt CLI, allow override argument. New files show in sidebar as normal buffers.
design.questions: Should we evolve git_diff_review or create new wt_review module? How to test Neovim UI plugin — no existing Lua test directory. Do we need plenary.nvim test setup?
